message:
fcp-mesh: forged-signature regression tests for decode-status + symbol-ack handlers

## Why

A memory-note audit flagged `MeshNode::handle_decode_status`,
`MeshNode::handle_symbol_ack`, and `MeshNode::handle_summary` as
handlers that didn't verify the inbound message signature. That audit
is stale — verification now lives at node.rs:1640 and node.rs:1664,
and `handle_summary` routes through `verify_summary_signature` at
node.rs:1342. But the negative-path coverage was incomplete: the two
existing `*_rejects_replay_to_different_recipient` tests exercise the
recipient-mismatch gate, and `handle_*_delegates_to_handler` /
`handle_*_increments_ack_metric` cover the happy path, but NEITHER
checked what happens when a valid Ed25519 signature is produced by a
key the local node did *not* register for that peer.

That's the exact shape of a silent-downgrade regression: a careless
crypto refactor that turns `verify()` into `Ok(())` (or that passes
the wrong key into the lookup) would slip past the entire existing
suite. These two tests close that guard.

## What

Two tests in `crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs`:

  - `handle_decode_status_rejects_forged_signature` — registers
    peer-1's `registered_key`, signs a `DecodeStatus` with a
    *different* `attacker_key`, and asserts the handler returns
    `PeerSignatureInvalid { message_kind: "decode status", .. }`
    plus that no ack was processed as a side-effect.
  - `handle_symbol_ack_rejects_forged_signature` — same shape for
    `SymbolAck`: forged-key signature, `PeerSignatureInvalid`
    rejection, and an explicit assertion that the acks-received
    metric does not bump.

Both pair with the existing replay/recipient-mismatch tests so the
three signature-related rejection paths (unsigned, wrong-recipient,
forged-key) each have a dedicated regression guard.

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p2-r1 cargo test -p fcp-mesh --lib \\
      rejects_forged_signature
  → 2/2 pass

Full `cargo test -p fcp-mesh` is green for the 1188 lib tests I
added into; the two pre-existing `coordinator::tests::acquire_*`
failures reproduce on clean main and are outside the scope of this
change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
